sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: ἱερὸν καθίζων τρίποδʼ ; ἐγὼ μὲν οὐ δοκῶ .
sections.translation: Seated on the holy tripod ? I do not think so .

sections.label: Section 981
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν πιθοίμην εὖ μεμαντεῦσθαι τάδε .
sections.translation: I cannot believe that this oracle was well prophesied .

sections.label: Section 982
sections.original: οὐ μὴ κακισθεὶς εἰς ἀνανδρίαν πεσῇ .
sections.translation: Do not become a coward and fall into unmanliness !

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἦ τὸν αὐτὸν τῇδʼ ὑποστήσω δόλον ;
sections.translation: Am I to devise the same crafty scheme for her ?

sections.label: Section 984
sections.original: ᾧ καὶ πόσιν καθεῖλες , Αἴγισθον κτανών .
sections.translation: The same death that you gave to her husband , Aegisthus .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: ἔσειμι · δεινοῦ δʼ ἄρχομαι προβλήματος
sections.translation: I will go in ; it is a dreadful task I am beginning and I will do dreadful things . If the gods approve , let it be ; to me the contest is bitter and also sweet .

sections.label: Section 988
sections.original: ἰώ ,
sections.translation: Hail , Queen of the land of Argos , child of Tyndareus ,

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: καὶ τοῖν ἀγαθοῖν ξύγγονε κούροιν
sections.translation: and sister of those two noble sons of Zeus who dwell in the fiery heavens among the stars , whose honored office it is to save mortals in the high waves . Welcome , I give you worship equal to the blessed gods

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: πλούτου μεγάλης τʼ εὐδαιμονίας .
sections.translation: for your wealth and great prosperity . Now is the time to pay our court to your fortunes . Welcome , o queen .

sections.label: Section 998
sections.original: ἔκβητʼ ἀπήνης , Τρῳάδες , χειρὸς δʼ ἐμῆς
sections.translation: Come out of the wagon , Trojan maids , and take my hand , that I may step down from the chariot .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: σκύλοισι μὲν γὰρ θεῶν κεκόσμηνται δόμοι
sections.translation: The homes of the gods are adorned with Phrygian spoils , but I have obtained these women , choice objects from the land of Troy , in return for the daughter whom I lost , a slight reward but an ornament to my house .

sections.label: Section 1004
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐγώ — δούλη γὰρ ἐκβεβλημένη
sections.translation: And , mother — for I live as a slave

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: δόμων πατρῴων δυστυχεῖς οἰκῶ δόμους —
sections.translation: in this miserable house , cast out from my father ’ s home — may I not take that blessed hand of yours ?

sections.label: Section 1007
sections.original: δοῦλαι πάρεισιν αἵδε , μὴ σύ μοι πόνει .
sections.translation: These slaves are here ; take no trouble on my account .

sections.label: Section 1008
sections.original: τί δʼ ; αἰχμάλωτόν τοί μʼ ἀπῴκισας δόμων ,
sections.translation: What ? You sent me away from home , a captive ; I was taken when my home was taken , like these ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ὡς αἵδε , πατρὸς ὀρφαναὶ λελειμμέναι .
sections.translation: all of us orphaned of a father .

sections.label: Section 1011
sections.original: τοιαῦτα μέντοι σὸς πατὴρ βουλεύματα
sections.translation: Well , your father laid such plots against those whom least of all he should have , his own family . I will tell you ; although when a woman gets an evil reputation , her tongue is bitter .

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: ὡς μὲν παρʼ ἡμῖν , οὐ καλῶς · τὸ πρᾶγμα δὲ
sections.translation: In my opinion , not rightly ; but it is correct for those who learn about the matter to hate , if it deserves hatred ; if not , why hate at all ? Now Tyndareus gave me to your father not so that I or any children I might bear should die .

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: κεῖνος δὲ παῖδα τὴν ἐμὴν Ἀχιλλέως
sections.translation: But that man went from the house , taking my child , with the persuasion of a marriage with Achilles , to Aulis which held the fleet ; and there he stretched Iphigenia over the pyre , and cut her white cheek . And if , as a cure for the capture of the city ,

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: ἢ δῶμʼ ὀνήσων τἄλλα τʼ ἐκσῴζων τέκνα ,
sections.translation: or as a benefit to his house , or to save his other children , he had killed one on behalf of many , I would have pardoned him . But , because Helen was lustful and the one who had her as a wife did not know how to punish the betrayer — for these reasons he destroyed my child .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ἐπὶ τοῖσδε τοίνυν καίπερ ἠδικημένη
sections.translation: Well , although I was wronged , I would not have been angry at this , nor would I have killed my husband . But he came back to me with a girl , raving and possessed , and put her in his bed , and had two brides at once in the same house .

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: μῶρον μὲν οὖν γυναῖκες , οὐκ ἄλλως λέγω ·
sections.translation: A woman is a foolish thing , I don ’ t deny it ; but , this granted , whenever a husband goes astray and rejects his own bed , the woman is likely to imitate her husband and find another love . And then in us the blame shines clearly ,

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: οἱ δʼ αἴτιοι τῶνδʼ οὐ κλύουσʼ ἄνδρες κακῶς .
sections.translation: while the men , who caused this , are not badly spoken of . Now if Menelaus had been secretly snatched from his home , should I have killed Orestes to save Menelaus , my sister ’ s husband ? How would your father have endured this ? And so isn ’ t it right for him to die

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: κτείνοντα χρῆν τἄμʼ , ἐμὲ δὲ πρὸς κείνου παθεῖν ;
sections.translation: when he had killed what was mine , since I would have suffered at his hands ? I killed him , I turned where indeed it was possible to go — to his enemies . For which one of your father ’ s friends would have joined me in his murder ? Speak , if you want to say anything , and make your retort with frankness ,

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: ὅπως τέθνηκε σὸς πατὴρ οὐκ ἐνδίκως .
sections.translation: in what way your father died unfairly .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original: δίκαιʼ ἔλεξας · ἡ δίκη δʼ αἰσχρῶς ἔχει .
sections.translation: Justly spoken , but this justice is shameful . For a woman should yield to her husband in all things , if she has sense ; anyone who does not agree does not come within the scope of what I say .

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: μέμνησο , μῆτερ , οὓς ἔλεξας ὑστάτους
sections.translation: Remember , mother , those last words of yours , giving me frankness towards you .

sections.label: Section 1057
sections.original: καὶ νῦν γέ φημι κοὐκ ἀπαρνοῦμαι , τέκνον .
sections.translation: I say it again , and I do not deny it , child .

sections.label: Section 1058
sections.original: ἆρα κλύουσα , μῆτερ , εἶτʼ ἔρξεις κακῶς ;
sections.translation: Then will you treat me badly , when you hear it ?

sections.label: Section 1059
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι , τῇ σῇ δʼ ἡδὺ προσθήσω φρενί .
sections.translation: No , no , I will give some pleasure to your heart .

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: λέγοιμʼ ἄν · ἀρχὴ δʼ ἥδε μοι προοιμίου ·
sections.translation: I will speak , and this is the beginning of my preface : oh , mother , I wish that you had a better heart . For though your beauty , and Helen ’ s , is worthy to bring you praise , yet you two were born true sisters , both frivolous , not worthy of Castor .

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: ἣ μὲν γὰρ ἁρπασθεῖσʼ ἑκοῦσʼ ἀπώλετο ,
sections.translation: She was carried off , willingly ruined ; and you have destroyed the bravest man in Greece , putting forward the excuse that you killed a husband for the sake of a child ; for they don ’ t know you so well as I do . You who , before your daughter ’ s death was decided ,

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: νέον τʼ ἀπʼ οἴκων ἀνδρὸς ἐξωρμημένου ,
sections.translation: as soon as your husband had started from home , were adorning the golden locks of your hair at the mirror . A wife who decks herself out for beauty , when her husband is gone from home — strike her off the list as worthless .

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: φαίνειν πρόσωπον , ἤν τι μὴ ζητῇ κακόν .
sections.translation: There is no need for her to show her pretty face out of doors , unless she is seeking some mischief . Of all the women in Hellas you were the only one I know to be joyful when Troy was fortunate , and with a clouded face when it was weaker , since you did not want Agamemnon to return from Troy .

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: καίτοι καλῶς γε σωφρονεῖν παρεῖχέ σοι ·
sections.translation: And yet it was in your power to be chaste , and rightly ; you had a husband , no worse than Aegisthus , whom Hellas chose to be its commander ; and when your sister Helen had done her work , it was possible for you to achieve great fame ,

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: παράδειγμα τοῖς ἐσθλοῖσιν εἴσοψίν τʼ ἔχει .
sections.translation: for the bad gives a standard of comparison to the good and provides a spectacle . But if , as you say , my father killed your daughter , what is the wrong I and and my brother have done you ? How was it that after you had killed your husband , you did not assign to us our father ’ s home ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: τἀλλότρια , μισθοῦ τοὺς γάμους ὠνουμένη ;
sections.translation: but you brought the goods belonging to another to bed , buying your marriage with wages ? And how is it that your husband is not exiled in the place of your son , nor has he died in my place , although he has killed me , alive , twice as much as my sister ? If murder , giving judgment , requites murder , your

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: καὶ παῖς Ὀρέστης πατρὶ τιμωρούμενοι ·
sections.translation: son Orestes and I must kill you to avenge our father . For if that was just , then so is this . Whoever marries a wicked woman , looking to wealth or noble birth , is a fool ; for humble wives , if chaste , are better in the home than great ones .

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: τύχη γυναικῶν ἐς γάμους . τὰ μὲν γὰρ εὖ ,
sections.translation: Chance is in the marriages of women ; among mankind , I see some fall out well , others not .

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: ὦ παῖ , πέφυκας πατέρα σὸν στέργειν ἀεί ·
sections.translation: Child , it was always your nature to love your father . This is what happens : some children are for their fathers , others in turn love their mothers more than a father .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: συγγνώσομαί σοι · καὶ γὰρ οὐχ οὕτως ἄγαν
sections.translation: I will forgive you ; for I do not rejoice so very much at what I have done , child .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα τῶν ἐμῶν βουλευμάτων ·
sections.translation: You , a woman who has just given birth — why is your body so unwashed and meanly clad ? Alas for my schemes !

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: ὡς μᾶλλον ἢ χρῆν ἤλασʼ εἰς ὀργὴν πόσιν .
sections.translation: I drove on in anger against my husband more than I should have .

sections.label: Section 1111
sections.original: ὀψὲ στενάζεις , ἡνίκʼ οὐκ ἔχεις ἄκη .
sections.translation: You sigh too late , when you have no remedy . My father is dead ; but why do you not recall that exile , your own wandering son ?

sections.label: Section 1114
sections.original: δέδοικα · τοὐμὸν δʼ , οὐχὶ τοὐκείνου , σκοπῶ .
sections.translation: l am afraid ; I am looking to my interests , not his .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: πατρὸς γάρ , ὡς λέγουσι , θυμοῦται φόνῳ .
sections.translation: For he is angry , they say , over the murder of his father .

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: τί δαὶ πόσιν σὸν ἄγριον εἰς ἡμᾶς ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: And why do you cause your husband to be cruel to me ?

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: τρόποι τοιοῦτοι · καὶ σὺ δʼ αὐθάδης ἔφυς .
sections.translation: Such are his ways . You have a stubborn nature also .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: ἀλγῶ γάρ · ἀλλὰ παύσομαι θυμουμένη .
sections.translation: Yes , for I am in distress . Yet I will cease from my anger .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐκεῖνος οὐκέτʼ ἔσται σοι βαρύς .
sections.translation: And then he will no longer be harsh to you .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: φρονεῖ μέγʼ · ἐν γὰρ τοῖς ἐμοῖς ναίει δόμοις .
sections.translation: He is proud ; for he lives in my home .

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original: ὁρᾷς ; ἀνʼ αὖ σὺ ζωπυρεῖς νείκη νέα .
sections.translation: You see ? Again you are rekindling new quarrels .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: σιγῶ · δέδοικα γάρ νιν ὡς δέδοικʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I am silent ; I fear him — as I fear him .

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: παῦσαι λόγων τῶνδε . ἀλλὰ τί μʼ ἐκάλεις , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: Stop this talk ! But why did you summon me , child ?

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: ἤκουσας , οἶμαι , τῶν ἐμῶν λοχευμάτων ·
sections.translation: You have heard , I suppose , that I have given birth ;

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: τούτων ὕπερ μοι θῦσον — οὐ γὰρ οἶδʼ ἐγώ —
sections.translation: in thanks for this , please sacrifice — for I do not know how — on the tenth day , as is the custom for the child . For I have no experience , being childless before .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: ἄλλης τόδʼ ἔργον , ἥ σʼ ἔλυσεν ἐκ τόκων .
sections.translation: This is work for another , the one who delivered you .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: αὐτὴ ʼλόχευον κἄτεκον μόνη βρέφος .
sections.translation: I was all alone in my labor and at the baby ’ s birth .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: οὕτως ἀγείτων οἶκος ἵδρυται φίλων ;
sections.translation: Is this household situated with no friends as neighbors ?

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original: πένητας οὐδεὶς βούλεται κτᾶσθαι φίλους .
sections.translation: No one is willing to have the poor as friends .
